nativeRange.habitat: Still or slow-moving warm water: coastal streams, ponds, swamps, canals, tidal ditches, and brackish estuaries; highly vegetated areas near the surface

decorPreferences: dense planting (Java fern, hornwort, water sprite, floating plants)
open swimming areas for male dorsal display
driftwood or rocks as territory markers

diet.notes: The Sailfin Molly feeds primarily on plant matter in the wild, grazing on algae, rotifers, and small crustaceans. In the aquarium, algae wafers, spirulina flakes, blanched vegetables such as zucchini and spinach, and vegetable-based flakes should make up the core of the diet. High-quality flake food, pellets, and frozen foods such as brine shrimp and daphnia are also readily accepted. Soft aquatic plants may occasionally be nibbled.

avoidWith: cherry shrimp and small dwarf shrimp (fry predation risk)
small fish that may be harassed by mature males

summary: The Sailfin Molly (Poecilia latipinna) is widely regarded as the largest member of the molly group, with mature males developing a dramatically tall dorsal fin used in courtship displays. The species tolerates brackish conditions well and generally does best in hard, alkaline water.
careNotes: The Sailfin Molly is widely regarded as the largest member of the Molly group, with mature males reaching up to 15 cm and developing a dramatically extended dorsal fin that is displayed during courtship. A tank of at least 150 L with a minimum 120 cm footprint has been recommended to give males adequate space to display and to house a typical trio (1 male, 2+ females). Maintain hard, alkaline water (gH 15–30, kH 8–20, pH 7.5–8.5); soft or acidic water has been associated with increased disease susceptibility and shortened lifespan. Mineral supplementation is advisable in soft-water areas. A 2:1 or 3:1 female-to-male ratio tends to reduce male harassment of females. The Sailfin Molly is euryhaline — it naturally inhabits coastal brackish and even marine waters, and many experienced keepers report improved health and reduced disease rates when a small amount of marine salt (e.g., SG 1.002–1.005) is added; this is not strictly required but is widely considered beneficial for long-term keeping. The trade name 'Marble Molly' refers to a mottled color morph of the same species. Sailfin Mollies have been crossed extensively with the Short-finned Molly (P. sphenops) to produce many of the hybrid commercial strains common in the trade; pure P. latipinna males are distinguishable by the large dorsal sail. Related entries: [molly] covers the smaller, short-finned common Molly (P. sphenops), and [balloon-molly] covers the selectively-bred compressed-body morph derived from P. sphenops.
breedingNotes: The Sailfin Molly is a prolific livebearer, with a gestation period of approximately 6–8 weeks and broods of up to 50 fry. Females can store sperm and produce multiple broods from a single mating. No parental care is provided, so dense planting substantially improves fry survival by giving the young fish cover from adults. Fry are born live and large enough to accept finely crushed flake and micro-pellets from birth. A 2–3:1 female-to-male ratio is generally recommended to reduce male harassment. Males begin developing the distinctive dorsal sail as they mature.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Poecilia latipinna
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided adult size (males 12.5 cm, females 11 cm), temperature 21–26°C, pH 7.0–8.5, hardness 15–35 dH, native range, biotope, diet, and breeding notes. Aquarium Co-Op (https://www.aquariumcoop.com/blogs/aquarium/molly-fish-care) used as primary trade source — no dedicated P. latipinna page exists; general molly guide supplemented sailfin-specific data (tank size 29–55 gal for larger species, temperature 24–27°C, salt tolerance noted).

additional.name: FishBase — Poecilia latipinna
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (males to 15 cm TL, females to 10 cm), temperature (20–28°C), native range (Cape Fear NC to Veracruz MX), habitat (still/slow warm coastal waters), taxonomy (Poeciliidae, Cyprinodontiformes), diet (algae, rotifers, small crustaceans). Size spread: SeriouslyFish max male 12.5 cm vs FishBase 15 cm — used 15 cm per user brief and FishBase morphological maximum.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Molly Fish Care
additional.notes: General molly care guide; supplemented sailfin-specific size/tank guidance. No dedicated P. latipinna page available. Temperature 24–27°C and tank size 29–55 gal (110–208 L) for larger molly species cited; 150 L chosen as conservative round value within that range per playbook guidance.

fish.breedingNotes: The Sailfin Molly is a highly prolific livebearer with a gestation period of approximately 6–8 weeks and broods of up to 50 fry. No specialized breeding setup is required beyond maintaining a 2–3:1 female-to-male ratio and providing dense planting for fry cover. Fry are born live and will accept micro-crushed flake food from birth.
